displayTitle: Nobody Fixes a Procedure at the Bench

beats.narration: At Level Two the written procedure stops being something you follow. It becomes something you review before it ever reaches the floor.

payload.title: REVIEW THE DRAFT
payload.sub: The procedure is where requirements become work.

beats.narration: E1417 demands a written procedure applicable to the part, approved by a certified Level Three, and submitted to the customer's engineering organization when required.
Completeness is partly arithmetic. Every parameter must sit inside the practice's limits — and minimums alone never pass, because the process fails both ways.
Then walk it twice. Pass one checks every element is present and in-limits. Pass two asks whether your shop can actually run it as written.
Verbs without numbers, materials by trade name, missing maximums — any one fails the draft. Revise it. Nobody fixes a procedure at the bench.

payload.sub: Improvising around a bad procedure is an uncontrolled process.
